title: 第 52 節
en: Here and Sv&r&.

We have no satisfactory etymology of heros.. said to be svarvat, or of Herakles, but why Here was called Here is not difficult to discover. We must not imagine that the Vedic poets can supply us with names for every one of the Greek deities, but much is gained if we can find in the Vedic poetry words and ideas that throw light on the names and concepts of Greek deities. If there was a name in the Veda accurately corresponding to Here, it would have been svara, that is, an adjective derived from svar with the feminine termination a, and lengthening, of the radical vowel. Now svar in the Veda is the name not only of the bright sun, but likewise of the bright sky. From it an adjective could be formed svarya, contracted to Surya, the recognised name for the sun in Sanskrit, while Surya is in the Veda a subordinate and feminine representative of the sunlight. If Zeus was Dyaus, the bright sky, what could be a more appropriate name for his wife than Svara, Here, the Dea Urania coelestis, originally, it may be, the bright air on which the sky rests ? It is easy to say, But there is no such goddess in the Veda. True, neither is there a goddess Hestia in the Veda, yet its etymological connection with the Sk. root vas is recognised by everybody, though on phonetic grounds alone it would be impossible to determine whether it was derived from the root vas, to shine, or the root vas, to dwell 1. A few more specimens 1 See Curtius, Grundztige, p. 399 ; Koth in K. Z., xix, p. 215 ; Chips, vol. iv, p. xxvii ; Fick, Indog. Worterb. s. v. veso, ich weile.
v] HERE AND SVAEA. 383 may be useful to show what we may have to expect whenever the etymology of mythological names is left in the hands of scholars who have nothing but contempt for Vedic writings.
Phoibos.

There can be no doubt that Phoibos, whatever its etymology may be, means in Greek pure, bright, radiant, and ^>oi/3aoj, to purify. Phoibos was originally the name of an independent deity, but when it became the epithet of Apollon, it meant, what Phoibe meant, when it became the epithet of Artemis, namely brilliant. It need not have been a name of the sun, or of Helios, but it certainly was a name that could only be applied to bright, matutinal, or solar deities. I am not ashamed to say that I know of no satisfactory etymology of <£oi/3o<?, but when we are told that cfiolfios meant originally a physician and that the name was applied to Apollon because from the beginning Apollon was the physician of the gods, I cannot follow. Apollon was not from the beginning a physician. On the contrary, he was looked upon as the healer of diseases because, first of all, and very much like the Vedic Kudra, he was supposed to send pestilence and other diseases by his arrows. He who could send sickness was supposed to be able to send healing also. Whether, as we are told, Houduv branched on from Apollon, or was identified with Apollon at a later time, is a question that I should not venture to answer. But why should <£oi/3o9 have meant a physician % Because it was derived, as we are told, from bhisha^. Bhisha^ in Sanskrit means to heal, bhisha(/a means healthy, bheshar/am, medicine. This 384 PHOIBOS. [CHAP.

root bhishary 1 is very obscure in Sanskrit, being an anomalous root of two syllables. Pott explained bhishakti as a compound abhi-shakti, meaning he heals. In Sanskrit, however, abhishac/ means really he curses. That might not be a serious objection, for abhi-shar/ might well have meant originally to stick something on a wound, or pass the hands over a person to heal him.

Prof. Bechtel, however, takes this root as a simple root and reduces bhish-na-(7, or bhish-a-^ to bhish(/, or rather bhesh^, originally bhoish</, and he sees in this bhoishf/ the etymon of <£oi/3o9. Passing over the phonetic difficulties, such as the dropping of the sibilant, and the change of a final g to b, what can we do with an etymology that would give to </kh/3o? the meaning of surgeon (^eipcop), but not of bright ? As I said before, I am not going to propose a new etymology of Phoibos, but considering the great similarity between the characters of Apollon and Puclra, as pointed out by Kuhn, Bhava, as another name of Rudra, would certainly lend itself to a change from <j>6j3os or cf>6fiios to (f>ol(3os, just as cpovos leads to (f)OLvos through </>oVio?.
Apollon.

And what shall we say to the new etymology of Apollon which derives this god of light from aTreikyj, threatening, or Latin ap-pelare ? Could a physical god, like Apollon, have in the first instance been called an addresser, from d^eWa, Ansprache (Appell) 1 We should at all events expect to be told what the connecting-links could have been between the son CD 1 Science of Thought, p. 350.
v] APOLLON. 385 of Zeus and Leto, the brother of Artemis, the Delios, and the Addresser, supposing even that such a meaning could be supported by stronger evidence than we have at present.
Ares.

If Ares(fi), another son of Zeus and Here, could be explained, as we are told, by simply spelling his name with a small a as aprjs, then why should not Xdpts be explained by x^/51^ Ceres by ceres, the Sabine word for bread, and Janus by janua ?
Artemis.

Again, if we are asked to derive Artemis, the sister of Apollon, from apre/x/if?, fresh, hale, one only wonders that the Greeks should ever have been in doubt as to the origin of the names of their gods, and particularly of that of Artemis, the goddess alev dSfja/JTr), always unwedded. How to account for dialectic forms such as "ApTafMis-LTos, and even 'Apra/^vrt must then be left entirely as an open question.
Aphrodite.

The most startling etymology, however, which Prof. Bechtel has presented to us is that of Aphrodite. That the Greeks thought of her as born from the foam of the sea, shows at all events, as well as her names of Brychia, Anadyomene, and Haligeneia, that her rising from the sea was compatible with the traditional conception of that ancient incarnation of beauty and loveliness. As wife of Hephaistos she is distinctly called Charis, one of the numerous representatives of the Dawn. Her heavenly nature is indicated by the names of Ourania and even of Here. Now we are told, however, and in a most persuasive VOL. I. C C 386 APHRODITE. [chap.

tone, that the first name given to this goddess of love was connected with fordus, pregnant, and because she encouraged love and marriage, she is supposed to have been celebrated and worshipped as the Pregnant Woman. Fortunately Greek sculptors did not take this view of her, and the very author of this etymology is evidently afraid of the consequences which it would involve. He qualifies it, therefore, as quite uncertain. But he adds, acfrpos may be a weak form of ve<f>po<;, kidney, though he fortunately passes other possibilities over in silence.

The only possibility which I can see is that this bright and beautiful goddess represented the Dawn, and was therefore by Homer considered worthy of such parents as Zeus and Dione, As rising from the sea, a kind of female Apam napat, she might, besides the name of Charis, Ourania, and Enalia, have received the name of Aphrogeneia, born from the froth of the sea, unless we take aphros in the sense which abhra has in Sanskrit, namely cloud or sky. This would represent her as what she is, one of the many daughters of the sky. All this shows at all events that Aphrodite was a beautiful creation of the Greek mind, however much it may afterwards have been contaminated by contact with similar goddesses of the East. To suppose that Astarte was the original of the name of Aphrodite would be the same as to take Moloch as the original of Zeus Meilichios.

I have given these few mythological etymologies as specimens of what we have to expect from scholars who scoff and sneer at every comparison between Greek and Vedic deities, and at every etymology v] APHRODITE. 387 that dares to appeal to Sanskrit roots. They dispose of a comparison such as Ouranos = Varuna by calling it a failure, without any attempt to prove it so, nay, they maintain that Erinys cannot be the same word as Sara^yu, because it is derived from the Greek ipivvco, to be angry. Would they derive SaKpv from SaKpvetv, or epcs from epi^eiv ?

If these are the best specimens of what is called Modern Philology, I confess that I still belong to the dark ages. I am delighted, no doubt, whenever the comparisons of my thological names are in strictest accordance with the phonetic rules that apply to nouns and verbs, but I should consider it simply pharisaical to object to such an equation as Vanma = Ovpavos considering the similarity, nay almost identity, of Varu^a with Ahura Mazda on one side, and of Varmia with Ovpavb<; euyou? virepOev, ao-Tepoeus, on the other.

So much may suffice to explain my present position with regard to phonetic rules when applied to proper names. I formerly agreed with Curtius that phonetic rules should be used against proper names with the same severity as against ordinary nouns and verbs (Grundziige, p. 1 20). I am now convinced that Benfey and others were right in protesting against this extreme view, very much on the strength of facts which could not be accounted for without placing a certain restriction on the universal sway of phonetic rules. I am glad to see Professor Victor Henry expressing the same conviction when he says : ' Mais les alterations de noms propres sont si aisees, et les causes en sont si fuyantes, qu'on ne peut en bonne justice exiger de la mythographie l'observation absolument rigoureuse de la phonetique '
c c 2 388 APHRODITE. [CHAP.
(Quelques Mythes naturalistes, p. 6). Of course this will be called backsliding and many other hard names, but in the end facts generally carry the day, even against scribes and pharisees.

Lest it should be supposed, however, that phonetic rules are like natural laws, and exceptions, entirely contra naturam. I add a few remarks on words which are not proper names, and which nevertheless offend against some fundamental phonetic law.

Let us take such common words as ovk, not, and K, out. They run counter to a well ascertained principle of the Greek language that no consonants these two cases e/c and ovk (before vowels or at the end of a sentence) are sufficient to upset a rule that seemed to be based on a physical inability on the part of the Greeks to pronounce a final muta, nor has it been possible to detect any reason why this rule should have been broken in these two cases only, particularly as there was e£ by the side of 4k, and ov and ovyi by the side of ovk.
Anomalous Words of a more Ancient Stratum.

Such anomalies occur most frequently in words of frequent usage, because the very frequency of their usage gave them the power to resist the levelling influences of later phonetic tendencies. In many languages such verbs as, to be, to go, to know, &c, nouns such as father, mother, daughter, &c, adjectives such as good and bad, are mostly irregular, simply because they have retained their ancient forms. Why should the a of nar-qp and pater, be i in Sk. pitar ? Why should [irJT-qp have the acute on the first, Sk. matar on the last syllable ? The apparent v] WORDS OF A MORE ANCIENT STRATUM. 389 irregularities of duhitar and OvyaTrjp have been discussed again and again, for the last time by Bartholomae in K. Z., xxvii, p. 206 ; yet in spite of all, no one ever doubted the common origin of these words. What can be more different, phonetically, than kv and $v, yet Sw^o? is but a variety of Kvefyas l ? What can be more certain than that e/c^rt and aexrjTL are connected with the Sk. vasat, yet there is as yet no explanation of the final 1 after the termination of the ablative ? Why did Sk. Aatvar, four, quatuor, lose its first syllable in Sk. turya instead of Aaturya, the fourth, and in rpdire^a instead of TeTpdne^a % Why is the suffix of Aaturtha, the fourth, th in Sanskrit, but t in Latin quartus and Greek Teraproq 2 ? All these anomalies, and a hundred more, have simply to be accepted, till more light can be thrown on them, but they could never induce us to doubt the real relationship of such words.

I fully recognise the dangers of such a theory, if it were made an excuse of every kind of phonetic licence, but we must learn to accept facts such as they are. The difference between historic and prehistoric phonetic laws has been recognised by the best scholars from Curtius to Brugmann and Joh. Schmidt, and there is of course no class of words which has a greater right to claim exception from the recognised historic phonetic laws than the oldest mythological names. If a substantial harmony between two characters in cognate languages and cognate mythologies has once been established, the slight phonetic differences which we observe, for instance, 1 On xve'cpas, \j/(4>as, 8v6(pos, see K. Z., xxxii, p. 357.
2 Brugmann, Grundriss, vol. ii, pp. 229, 473.
390 WORDS or A more ancient stratum. [chap.
zh: 我注意到提供的英文原文「Here and Sv&r&.」似乎不完整或含有編碼錯誤。 根據翻譯規則，我只應輸出翻譯後的繁體中文。然而，這段文本無法確定其完整意義。 請提供完整且清晰的英文原文，我將據以提供準確的繁體中文翻譯。

我們對於英雄神（heros）沒有令人滿意的語源學解釋，據說源自 svar-vat，對於赫拉克勒斯（Herakles）亦然，但為何赫拉（Here）被如此稱呼並不難發現。我們不應想像吠陀詩人能為希臘諸神的每一位提供名字，但如果我們能在吠陀詩歌中找到詞彙和觀念，足以闡明希臘諸神的名稱和概念，那就大有所獲。若吠陀中存在一個名稱精確對應赫拉，那應當是 svara，即由 svar 派生的形容詞，帶有陰性詞尾 a，並伴隨詞根元音的長化。現在 svar 在吠陀中不僅是光明太陽的名稱，同樣也是光明天空的名稱。由此可以形成形容詞 svarya，縮合為 Surya，即梵文中公認的太陽名稱，而 Surya 在吠陀中則是陽光的次要的、陰性的代表。若宙斯（Zeus）是特尤斯（Dyaus），即光明的天空，那麼什麼名稱比 Svara、赫拉更適合作為其妻子的名稱呢——天上的女神（Dea Urania coelestis），或許原初是天空所依托的光明氣體？有人易於說道：「但吠陀中並不存在這樣的女神。」誠然，吠陀中也不存在赫斯提亞（Hestia）女神，然而它與梵文詞根 vas 的詞源學聯繫為人所公認，儘管單從音韻學角度而言，要判定它究竟源自詞根 vas（發光）或詞根 vas（居住）是不可能的。再舉數例
這可能有助於說明，每當神話學名稱的詞源學研究落入那些對吠陀典籍毫無敬畏之心的學者手中時，我們可能會面臨什麼樣的局面。
弗伊博斯（Phoibos）。

毫無疑問，φοῖβος（Phoibos）無論其詞源如何，在希臘文中意為純淨、光亮、燦爛，而 φοιβάω（phoibaō）則意為淨化。φοῖβος 最初是一位獨立神祇的名稱，但當它成為阿波羅（Apollon）的修飾詞時，它的意義與 φοίβη（Phoibe）成為阿耳忒彌斯（Artemis）的修飾詞時相同，即輝煌。它不必非得是太陽或赫利俄斯（Helios）的名稱，但它肯定是一個只能用於光亮的、晨曦的或太陽神祇的名稱。我不恥於坦言，我對 φοῖβος 不知有何令人滿意的詞源解釋，但當我們被告知 φοῖβος 最初意為醫者，且該名稱之所以被應用於阿波羅，是因為從一開始阿波羅就是眾神的醫者時，我無法苟同。阿波羅從一開始並非醫者。相反地，他被看作疾病的治癒者，是因為首先且非常類似於吠陀的魯陀（Rudra），人們認為他會透過他的箭矢散播瘟疫和其他疾病。能夠送出疾病的人被認為也能夠送出治癒。至於如我們所被告知的，赫卡塔埃俄斯（Houtduv）是否從阿波羅分出，或是在後來的時代與阿波羅相混同，這是一個我不敢妄下結論的問題。但為什麼 φοῖβος 應當意為醫者呢？因為據我們所被告知的，它衍生自 bhisha^。梵文中的 bhisha^ 意為治癒，bhisha(/a 意為健康，bheshar/am 意為藥物。
根詞 bhishary¹ 在梵文中極為晦澀，是一個異常的雙音節根詞。波特（Pott）將 bhishakti 解釋為複合詞 abhi-shakti，意為「他治癒」。然而在梵文中，abhishac/ 實際上意為「他詛咒」。這或許不是一個嚴重的異議，因為 abhi-shar/ 本來就可能意為將某物塗抹於傷口上，或將雙手按於某人身上以治癒他。

貝希特爾教授卻將此詞根視為簡單詞根，並將 bhish-na-（7 或 bhish-a-^ 簡化為 bhish(/，或更確切地說為 bhesh^，原本為 bhoish</，他在此 bhoishf/ 中看到了 φοῖβος 的詞源。撇開語音上的困難不談，如脫落輔音 s 以及詞尾 g 變為 b 這類問題，我們對一部詞源學著作能做什麼呢？該著作會賦予 φοῖβος 以「外科醫生」（χειρουργός）的意義，卻未能給出「光明的」之義？如我之前所說，我並無意提出 Phoibos 的新詞源，但考慮到 Kuhn 所指出的阿波羅與普德拉（Puclra）二神性格的巨大相似性，以及作為盧陀羅另一名號的婆婆（Bhava），它確實足以促成從 φόβος 或 φόμιος 到 φοῖβος 的演變，正如 φόνος 經由 φόνιος 而導致 φοῖνος 一般。
阿波羅（Apollon）。
那麼我們應該如何看待阿波羅（Apollon）的新詞源說呢？這種說法將這位光明之神追溯至梵文 *treikyj*（威脅）或拉丁文 *ap-pelare*？一位物質神祇，如阿波羅，在最初階段是否可能被稱為「說話者」，源自梵文 *dêva*、德文 *Ansprache*（呼喚）？¹無論如何，我們應該期望被告知，在兒子⁽ᶜᵈ⁾與¹《思想科學》，第 350 頁。
v] 阿波羅（APOLLON）。385 特尤斯與勒托之子，阿耳忒彌斯之兄，得利亞人之神，以及「祈禱者」，即使假設這樣的涵義能得到比我們目前所掌握更有力的證據支持。
亞瑞斯（Ares）。
假若阿瑞斯（Ἄρης）是宙斯和赫拉的另一個兒子，據稱可以簡單地將其名字小寫拼作 aprjs 就能解釋，那麼為何不能將克洛諾斯（Χρόνος）解釋為 χρόνος，將刻瑞斯（Ceres）解釋為 ceres（薩賓語中「麵包」一詞），以及將雅努斯（Janus）解釋為 janua？
阿爾特彌斯。

再者，如果我們被要求從 ἀπρέσκει（fresh, hale，新鮮、強健）來推導阿耳忒彌斯（Artemis）——阿波羅的妹妹——那就只能令人驚訝：希臘人對於自己諸神之名的起源，尤其是對於阿耳忒彌斯這位女神之名的起源，為何竟然曾經感到困惑，她乃是永遠未婚的女神（ἀλέα δούφα/JTr）。至於如何解釋 "Ἀρταμΐτος" 和甚至 'Ἀρταμέντι 等方言形式，則必須完全被擱置為一開放問題。
阿芙羅黛蒂。

然而，貝希特爾教授（Prof. Bechtel）向我們提出的最令人驚異的詞源，是關於阿佛洛狄忒（Aphrodite）的。希臘人認為她由海的泡沫而生，這一點至少表明，她名字中的布律基亞（Brychia）、阿那呂歐美涅（Anadyomene）和海麗革涅亞（Haligeneia），都與那種古老的關於美與可愛之具體化的傳統觀念相容。作為赫費斯托斯（Hephaistos）的妻子，她被明確地稱為卡里斯（Charis），此為眾多晨曦女神（the Dawn）代表之一。她的天界本性由烏拉尼亞（Ourania）這個名字，甚至由赫拉（Here）這個名字所暗示。然而現在我們受到告知，而且以最令人信服的方式，
愛神的第一個名號與 fordus（懷孕）相連，而且因為她鼓勵愛與婚姻，人們推測她曾被尊崇並膜拜為懷孕婦女。所幸希臘雕塑家並未採納這種觀點，而這個詞源說的作者本人顯然害怕其可能導致的後果。因此他將之限定為相當不確定的說法。但他補充說，ἀφροδίτη 可能是 νεφρός（腎臟）的弱化形式，儘管他所幸對其他可能性隻字未提。

在我看來，唯一的可能是這位光彩照人、美麗動人的女神代表了晨曦，因此被荷馬認為配得上宙斯（Zeus）和狄俄涅（Dione）這樣的雙親。作為從海洋中升起者，某種意義上的女性阿帕姆‧納帕特（*Apam napat*），她除了獲得卡瑞斯（*Charis*）、烏剌尼亞（*Ourania*）和埃那利亞（*Enalia*）等名號外，還可能獲得了阿芙洛狄忒（*Aphrogeneia*）的名號——海洋泡沫的生成者——除非我們將 *aphros* 理解為梵文 *abhra* 的含義，即雲或天空。這樣的話，她就代表了她的本質，即眾多天空之女中的一位。所有這一切無論如何都表明，阿芙羅狄忒是希臘精神創造的產物，儘管她後來可能因與東方相似女神的接觸而受到污染。若認為阿斯塔特（Astarte）是阿芙羅狄忒名號的原型，這就如同將莫洛克（Moloch）視為宙斯‧麥利基奧斯（Zeus Meilichios）的原型一樣荒謬。

我之所以舉述這些少數的神話詞源學例證，乃是為了展示那些嘲笑並貶抑希臘神祇與吠陀神祇之間每一項比較、以及每一項敢於訴諸梵文詞根的詞源學的學者們將面對的事物。他們用「失敗」一詞來駁斥如烏剌諾斯（Ouranos）= 伐樓那（Varuna）這樣的比較，而無須任何證明，不，他們甚至主張厄里尼斯（Erinys）不可能與莎剌由（Sarayu）是同一個詞，理由是它衍生自希臘文 ἐρίννυω（意為「發怒」）。那麼他們是否會由 δακρύνειν 來衍生 δάκρυ，或由 ἐπιέναι 來衍生 ἔπος？

如果這些就是所謂「現代語言學」的最佳典範，我必須坦承自己仍然屬於蒙昧時代。誠然，當我對神聖名稱的比較符合適用於名詞和動詞的最嚴格音韻規則時，我感到欣喜，但我認為對於 Varu**ṇ**a = Οὐρανός 這樣的等式提出反對，實在過於拘泥於字面；考慮到一方面 Varu**ṇ**a 與 Ahura Mazda 幾乎完全相同，另一方面 Varu**ṇ**a 與 Οὐρανός，εὐρυχόρευς, ἀστερόεις 的相似性——或者說幾乎同一性——這樣做未免顯得法利賽人般的狹隘。

以上足以說明我目前對於語音規則應用於專有名詞時的立場。我從前曾同意柯蒂烏斯（Curtius）的看法，認為應該以對待普通名詞和動詞同樣的嚴格程度來對待專有名詞的語音規則（《基礎》，第120頁）。但現在我確信班費（Benfey）及其他學者的抗議是正當的。我之所以改變看法，很大程度上是基於某些事實，如果不對語音規則的普遍適用性加以某種限制，便無法解釋這些事實。我很高興看到維克多‧亨利教授（Victor Henry）也表達了同樣的信念，他說：「但專有名詞的變化是如此容易，其原因又是如此難以捉摸，所以不能公正地要求神話記載學對於語音學的絕對嚴格遵守。」（*Mais les alterations de noms propres sont si aisees, et les causes en sont si fuyantes, qu'on ne peut en bonne justice exiger de la mythographie l'observation absolument rigoureuse de la phonetique*）
（Quelques Mythes naturalistes, p. 6）。當然這會被稱為背道而馳和許多其他刺耳的名詞，但最終事實通常會戰勝一切，即使是對抗文士和法利賽人也不例外。
然而，為免有人以為語音規則如同自然法則，異例完全違反自然（contra naturam），我特此補充數語，論述某些並非專名的詞彙，儘管如此仍然違犯某些基礎語音法則。

讓我們以「οὐκ」（not，非）和「ἐκ」（out，出）這樣的常見詞彙為例。它們違反了希臘語中一條經過充分確認的原則，即在這兩種情況下，沒有輔音這兩個詞 ἐκ 和 οὐκ（在母音前或句尾）足以推翻一條似乎基於希臘人在生理上無法發音末位塞音（muta）的規則。儘管如此，人們仍未能找到任何理由說明為什麼此規則只在這兩種情況下被打破，尤其是當時 ἐξ 與 ἐκ 並存，οὖ 和 οὖν 也與 οὐκ 並存的情況下。
異常詞彙層——更古老的地層

這類異常現象在頻繁使用的詞彙中出現得最為頻繁，正因為它們使用頻率高，反而賦予它們抵抗後來語音傾向之平整化影響的力量。在許多語言中，諸如「是」、「去」、「知」等動詞，諸如「父親」、「母親」、「女兒」等名詞，諸如「好」、「壞」等形容詞，多半都是不規則的，單純因為它們保留了古代的形態。為何 nar-qp 和 pater 中的 a，在梵文（Sk.）中會是 pitar 中的 i？為何 [irJT-qp 在第一音節上有銳重音，而梵文 matar 在最後一個音節上？duhitar 和 θvyάτηρ 看似不規則的情形已被論述過無數次，最近一次由巴特洛美在《比較語言學雜誌》（K. Z.）第二十七卷第二〇六頁討論過；然而儘管如此，沒有人曾懷疑這些詞的共同起源。什麼比得上 κν 和 σύ 在語音上差異更大，然而 σύνω？ 卻不過是 Κυέγας 的一個變體罷了？什麼比得上 ἐκ-εἰμι 和 αεκ-ητι 與梵文 vasat 相關聯更加確定呢，但迄今為止仍無法解釋格位詞尾之後的末尾 l？梵文 Aatvar「四」、quatuor 為何在梵文 turya 中喪失了首個音節，而不是 Aaturya「第四個」，以及在 τρίπε?α 中而不是 τετράπε?α？梵文 Aaturtha「第四個」的詞尾為何在梵文中是 th，但在拉丁文 quartus 和希臘文 Τέταρτος 中卻是 t？所有這些異常現象以及百般其他現象，暫時只能予以接受，待能對其投以更多光照，但它們決不能誘使我們懷疑此類詞彙的真實關係。

我充分認識到這種理論的危險性，倘若它被當作藉口來為各種語音上的放縱開脫，但我們必須學會接受事實如其本然。歷史時期與史前時期語音法則之間的差異已為從柯蒂烏斯（Curtius）到布魯格曼和約翰‧施密特等最傑出的學者所承認，而且顯然沒有哪一類詞彙比最古老的神話名稱更有權利要求豁免於公認的歷史語音法則。一旦在同源語言和同源神話之間建立起實質上的和諧，我們所觀察到的輕微語音差異——例如在ὑψέρας、ψῦχας、ὄνθρος等方面的差異，見《語言學期刊》第三十二卷，第三五七頁——就不足為奇了。
2 Brugmann，《概論》，第二卷，頁229、473。
更古老的地層。〔章
